name: Use Case 3: AI-Driven Tables, Figures, and Listings (TFL) Generation
title: CDISC Case 3 - ARS Reporting Event + ADaM to traceable TFLs
description: Execute a CDISC ARS v1.0 Reporting Event against ADaM to produce Tables, Figures, Listings and an ARD with end-to-end objective-to-result traceability. The work is decomposed into single-responsibility steps: deterministic binding (bind ARS refs to ADaM headers), deterministic classification + recipe planning, a deterministic recipe run for the standard safety outputs, an AI step scoped ONLY to drafting the custom efficacy programs, a tight human review of just those programs, then deterministic assembly + traceability. Artifacts land in /output.

steps.name: Provide inputs
steps.description: Confirm the inputs for this run. By default it executes the bundled CDISCPILOT01 reference (the ARS v1.0 Reporting Event from Case 2 + its ADaM). Leave blank to use the reference.

params.name: inputNote
params.description: Optional note (e.g. which study / ARS this run uses). Blank = bundled CDISCPILOT01 reference.

steps.name: Stage inputs
steps.description: Deterministic. Resolve the Reporting Event + ADaM from the upload, or fall back to the bundled CDISCPILOT01 reference. Writes /workspace/reporting_event.json and /workspace/adam/.

steps.name: Bind ARS to ADaM
steps.description: Deterministic. Walk the ARS analysisSets, dataSubsets, groupings and each Analysis; resolve every dataset.variable / WhereClause against the actual ADaM column headers. Writes /workspace/bindings.json (resolved bindings) and /workspace/unbound.json (any reference that does not resolve - a traceability gap surfaced early, never silently dropped).

steps.name: Classify outputs (standard vs custom)
steps.description: Deterministic. For each ARS Output decide standard (a validated recipe covers its shape: demographics, categorical/continuous summary, hierarchical AE by SOC/PT) vs custom (needs a fitted model or bespoke display: ANCOVA, Kaplan-Meier). Classification is a fixed rules table, not a judgement call. Writes /workspace/coverage.json.

steps.name: Run standard outputs (validated recipes)
steps.description: Deterministic, no LLM. For every output classified 'standard', call the fixed recipe library (cards/cardx/gtsummary) with ARS-derived bindings, emitting the long-skinny ARD (/workspace/ard/<id>.csv) and the rendered table (/workspace/tfl/<id>.html). 'Almost all safety outputs for free.'

steps.name: Draft custom efficacy programs
steps.description: AI - scoped ONLY to the custom outputs. For each output classified 'custom' in coverage.json, draft a standalone R program (ANCOVA via lm+emmeans, KM via survival), run it, and repair until it emits the same long-skinny ARD contract + a rendered display. Preserves the ARS analysis_id/operation_id on every row so lineage reconstructs. Writes /workspace/{ard,tfl,code}/<id>.* and updates coverage.json (status + repairs) for each custom output, leaving the standard outputs untouched. Adapts the proven reference /app/container/draft_custom.R. On a revise re-entry, reads the reviewer comment and fixes only the affected outputs.

steps.name: Review drafted programs
steps.description: Review only the AI-drafted custom efficacy programs (/workspace/code/*.R) and their rendered displays; coverage.json lists each custom output with its status and any repair notes. Approve to assemble, or request changes to send the run back to Draft custom with your comment. The deterministic standard outputs need no review.

params.name: comment
params.description: What to change (required when requesting changes).

steps.name: Assemble ARD + Traceability
steps.description: Deterministic. Coverage gate (every ARS Output has an ARD + a rendered file, else fail). Consolidate all /workspace/ard/*.csv into one long-skinny /output/ard.csv; write each value back into Analysis.results[] of /output/reporting_event_with_results.json; build the objective->endpoint->result graph (/output/trace_graph.json) and the interactive /output/traceability.html; write /output/manifest.json.

steps.name: Propose skill lesson from review feedback
steps.description: AI. Self-learning loop. Read /workspace/review_feedback.jsonl (the reviewer comments Draft custom captured on each revise of Review programs this run), the approved custom programs (/workspace/code/*.R), coverage.json, and the current draft-custom-programs lessons-learned.md, and distil durable, skill-general lessons — the guidance that would have made the agent draft it right the first time. Emits ONLY a new append-block (never rewrites the file) to /output/result.json as { hasLessons, lessonAppendMarkdown, prTitle, prBody, summary }. When review_feedback.jsonl is absent or empty (first-pass approve, no revisions) emits hasLessons=false and no lesson, so the next step opens no PR.

steps.name: Open skill-lesson PR
steps.description: Deterministic. If propose-skill-update produced a lesson (hasLessons=true), fresh-clone SKILL_REPO with GITHUB_TOKEN, append lessonAppendMarkdown to plugins/cdisc-case-3/skills/draft-custom-programs/references/lessons-learned.md, branch skill-lesson/<runId>, commit as Mediforce Bot, push, and open a PR against main via the GitHub API. hasLessons=false (or byte-identical file) => no PR, clean exit with prCreated=false. Idempotent on retry: force-pushes the branch and reuses an existing open PR.
